name: Revillallanos y Valdelamediana — circular through the Valderejo Natural Park
name_original: Revillallanos y Valdelamediana — circular por el Parque Natural de Valderejo
region_intro: Hiking around Jurisdicción De San Zadornil offers diverse landscapes within the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park, a transitional zone between Mediterranean and Atlantic climates. The region features significant geological formations, including high limestone reliefs and gorges carved by rivers like the Purón. Dense forests of beech, pine, oak, and ancient chestnut groves characterize much of the terrain, providing varied environments for exploration. Best hiking trails around Jurisdicción De San Zadornil The most popular hiking route is Revillallanos y Valdelamediana — circular through the Valderejo Natural Park, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Desfiladero del Río Purón y Alto de Revillallanos - desde Herrán — circular por el Parque Natural de Valderejo, a difficult 9.9 miles (16.0 km) path. This challenging trail navigates the Purón River gorge and ascends to the Alto de Revillallanos. Local hikers also love the Ribera – Purón River Gorge loop from Herrán, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through the Purón River Gorge, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Jurisdicción De San Zadornil is defined by limestone peaks, river gorges, and diverse forests, including ancient chestnut groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Jurisdicción De San Zadornil are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Jurisdicción De San Zadornil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Jurisdicción De San Zadornil?
A: Jurisdicción De San Zadornil offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 80 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging ascents, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Jurisdicción De San Zadornil?
A: The region is characterized by diverse terrain within the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park. You'll encounter significant geological formations, including high limestone reliefs and gorges carved by rivers. Dense forests of beech, pine, oak, and ancient chestnut groves are also prominent features, offering varied environments for exploration.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several gentle hiking options. The area provides easy walks through natural landscapes, including forest routes. Approximately 16 of the available routes are rated as easy, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in Jurisdicción De San Zadornil?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more demanding experience, there are 18 routes rated as difficult. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045956074/desfiladero-del-rio-puron-y-alto-de-revillallanos-desde-herran-circular-por-el-parque-natural-de-valderejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero del Río Purón y Alto de Revillallanos - desde Herrán&lt;/a&gt;, which navigates the Purón River gorge and ascends to the Alto de Revillallanos.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Jurisdicción De San Zadornil are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052375249/revillallanos-y-valdelamediana-circular-through-the-valderejo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Revillallanos y Valdelamediana — circular through the Valderejo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.7-mile trail.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I discover while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purón River Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4004660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo de Leron&lt;/a&gt;, or ascend peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4244321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humion&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Jurisdicción De San Zadornil?
A: While specific regulations can vary within the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park, generally, dogs are welcome on trails, especially if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage or park guidelines for any specific restrictions in certain areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Jurisdicción De San Zadornil?
A: Due to its transitional climate between Mediterranean and Atlantic, the region offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with vibrant flora and comfortable temperatures. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the shaded forest routes.

Q: Are there opportunities to see wildlife during a hike?
A: Yes, the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park is home to significant wildlife, including 128 vertebrate species. Keep an eye out for mammals like wildcats, otters, and roe deer. Birdwatchers might spot golden eagles, Bonelli's eagles, griffon vultures, and eagle owls.

Q: Where can I find parking for the trailheads?
A: Many trailheads in Jurisdicción De San Zadornil, especially those near villages like Herrán, offer designated parking areas. It's recommended to check the specific route details on komoot for information on parking availability at the starting point of your chosen hike.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Jurisdicción De San Zadornil?
A: The trails in Jurisdicción De San Zadornil are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, well-maintained paths, and the stunning natural beauty of the region.

Q: Are there any specific forest types or unique trees I might encounter?
A: The region is renowned for its diverse forests. You'll find ancient chestnut groves, particularly along the PRC-BU 100 trail near Arroyo, as well as extensive beech and Scots pine forests. Holm oaks and junipers are also common, especially on south-facing slopes.

Q: Can I find hikes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the area features natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1871481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. While not every trail leads directly to a waterfall, some routes may pass by or offer detours to these scenic spots.

Q: Are there any routes that explore river gorges?
A: The region is famous for its impressive river gorges. The Purón River has carved spectacular canyons, and you can explore them on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10508261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera – Purón River Gorge loop from Herrán&lt;/a&gt;, which offers an immersive experience through this dramatic landscape.
